description_long: This object consists of a central wooden handle with a cluster of five cylindrical metal canisters attached at the top, arranged in a circular pattern. The canisters are matte black and feature white stenciled German text, suggesting a military or historical context, likely referencing a World War II-era explosive device. The wooden handle is smooth and light brown, contrasting with the dark metal of the canisters. The canisters are tightly grouped, with their bases joined together, and the tops are flat and slightly domed. The overall construction appears robust, with the canisters likely containing explosive material and the handle designed for manual deployment. The object is utilitarian, with no visible electronics, wires, or digital components.
description: A cluster of five matte black cylindrical canisters with white stenciled text, mounted on a single wooden handle, resembling a historical hand-thrown bomb.
description_view_0: The cluster of five canisters is clearly visible, with the wooden handle extending downward; the stenciled text is partially readable.
description_view_1: The canisters are seen head-on, showing the circular arrangement and the central handle; text is visible on the front canisters.
description_view_2: The side view highlights the depth of the canister cluster and the length of the handle, with two canisters in the foreground.
description_view_3: The rear of the canister cluster is visible, with the handle centered and the back canister most prominent.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The object appears to be manually triggered, likely by striking or pulling a fuse or igniter located within the canister cluster, as no external buttons or switches are visible.
